name: bars-strand
description: Manage BARS strands - isolated work contexts that track development tasks with fork-space boundaries, GitHub issue linking, and status tracking. Perfect for organizing feature work, bug fixes, experiments, and research.

skills.name: create-strand
skills.description: Create a new development strand to track work on a feature, bugfix, experiment, or other task. Each strand has a unique name, description, type (feature/bugfix/experiment/research/refactor/docs), and status (active/paused/completed/archived).
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py create {name} --description '{description}' --type {type} --json

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the strand (lowercase-with-hyphens recommended)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Human-readable description of the strand's purpose and scope

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of work: feature (new functionality), bugfix (fix existing issues), experiment (proof-of-concept), research (investigation), refactor (code improvement), docs (documentation)

skills.name: list-strands
skills.description: List all strands in the repository. Optionally filter by status (active/paused/completed/archived) or type (feature/bugfix/experiment/research/refactor/docs). Use this to see what work is in progress, review completed work, or find paused tasks.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py list --json {filters}

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: active (currently working), paused (blocked/waiting), completed (finished), archived (historical reference)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by type: feature, bugfix, experiment, research, refactor, docs

skills.name: show-strand
skills.description: Show detailed information about a specific strand including its description, status, type, created/updated timestamps, linked GitHub issues, and fork-space configuration. Use this to review a strand's complete context.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py show {name} --json

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Strand identifier to show details for

skills.name: update-strand
skills.description: Update a strand's status or description. Use this to mark work as completed, pause blocked work, resume paused work, or update the description as scope evolves.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py update {name} {updates} --json

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status: active (resume/start work), paused (block/postpone), completed (finish), archived (move to history)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: New description text to replace existing description

skills.name: link-strand-to-issue
skills.description: Link a strand to a GitHub issue for traceability. This connects your development work to issue tracking, making it easy to see which strand addresses which issue. Multiple issues can be linked to one strand.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py link {name} {issue_url} --json

skills.name: delete-strand
skills.description: Permanently delete a strand. Use this to remove obsolete or incorrectly created strands. This action cannot be undone. Consider marking as archived instead if you want to keep the record.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py delete {name} --yes

skills.name: check-strand-config
skills.description: Check the BARS Strand system configuration for issues including API provider setup, API key availability, and configuration validity. Use this to troubleshoot setup problems or verify configuration before creating strands.
skills.command: cd {repo_root} && uv run python cli/bars-strand/bars_strand.py config check
